{"problem_standardized_math":"Let \\(\\{z_k\\}\\) be the sequence defined by \\((12.30)\\). Verify that, for the function \\(f(x)=x_1+x_2\\), one has\n\\[\nf(z_{k+1})>f(z_k), \\qquad k=2,3,\\ldots.\n\\]\nHint: Consider the trajectory\n\\[\nz(s)=\\bigl(-\\sqrt{2-1/s^2},\\,-1/s\\bigr)^T\n\\]\nand show that the function \\(h(s)\\stackrel{\\mathrm{def}}{=}f(z(s))\\) satisfies \\(h'(s)>0\\) for all \\(s\\ge 2\\)."}
